originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&len` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_GZIP true)
     (= ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH false)
     (= BB_MMU false))
model:
(define-fun BB_MMU () Bool
  false)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_GZIP () Bool
  true)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH () Bool
  false)
reasoning: send_headers(): `len` accumulates sprintf return values at L25 (and L30/L36 depending on config), but the final printf at L38 uses iobuf directly — len is never used after its last assignment. Dead store. content_gzip (L35) is only declared when GZIP=T; GZIP=F causes a compile error at L35. So the dead store requires GZIP=T. BASIC_AUTH=F and BB_MMU=F are not required: the dead store exists regardless of these flags (BASIC_AUTH only adds one more len update; BB_MMU affects whether send_headers is reachable at runtime, not its static analysis). No '// ERROR' → new bug not in VBDB. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
